content.problemStatement:
Array PPP is said to **match** array QQQ, if array QQQ can be transformed to PPP by the use of the following action multiple times:


Choose index 1‚Č§i‚Č§‚ą£Q‚ą£1 \le i \le |Q|1‚Č§i‚Č§‚ą£Q‚ą£, and an element XXX such that X‚Č†QiX \ne Q_iXÓÄ†=Qi‚Äč, and then insert XXX after index iii in array QQQ.
Notably, you cannot insert any element before the first index in QQQ.

You are given 222 arrays AAA of length NNN and BBB of length MMM.

Find the number of subarrays of AAA that **match** array BBB, i.e. the number of subarrays of AAA that can be formed from array BBB with the above operation.

Formally, compute the number of pairs of integers (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) such that A[L,R]A[L, R]A[L,R] matches BBB.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN and MMM - the size of the arrays AAA and BBB.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,...,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains MMM integers - B1,B2,...,BMB_1, B_2, ..., B_MB1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,...,BM‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output the number of subarrays of AAA that match BBB.
constraints: The sum of NNN does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105 over all test cases.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The only subarray of AAA that matches BBB is the subarray formed by (L=2,R=3)(L = 2, R = 3)(L=2,R=3), i.e. [1,2][1, 2][1,2]. This is already equal to BBB without any operations.
Test Case 2 : The following subarrays match BBB:

(L=1,R=2)(L = 1, R = 2)(L=1,R=2) : The subarray is [1,2][1, 2][1,2] which is already equal to BBB.
(L=1,R=3)(L = 1, R = 3)(L=1,R=3) : The subarray is [1,2,1][1, 2, 1][1,2,1] which can be formed with one operation of i=2,X=1i = 2, X = 1i=2,X=1. Note that X‚Č†2X \ne 2XÓÄ†=2.
(L=1,R=3)(L = 1, R = 3)(L=1,R=3) : The subarray is [1,2,1,2][1, 2, 1, 2][1,2,1,2] which can be formed with the operations of i=2,X=1i = 2, X = 1i=2,X=1 and then i=3,X=2i = 3, X = 2i=3,X=2.
(L=3,R=4)(L = 3, R = 4)(L=3,R=4) : The subarray is [1,2][1, 2][1,2], already equal to BBB.
